articles.title: Biden rallies Democrats, slams Trump at Maryland Democratic Party gala
articles.description: HANOVER — Former President Joe Biden embraced the theme of the Maryland Democratic Party’s “Fight Back & Win” gala Saturday night, urging state Democrats to stand up to President Donald Trump and his administration.

articles.title: Taste, Smell Disturbances Seen in GLP-1 Users
articles.description:
(MedPage Today) -- Use of GLP-1 receptor agonists was associated with a higher risk of smell and taste disturbances among adults with type 2 diabetes, an analysis of electronic health record (EHR) data suggested.

articles.description: A report about unusual access to an experimental obesity drug has raised interest in the condition pulmonary hypertension.

articles.title: The mysterious case of Eli Lilly’s obesity drug
articles.description: Who got exclusive access to Eli Lilly's highly anticipated obesity candidate? And why are drugmakers spending so much on acquisitions? Find out on this week's "Readout LOUD."

articles.description: The unusual access to an experimental drug has captured the attention of the American public as well.

articles.title: Feds: Maryland school policies on transgender students, athletes may violate Title IX
articles.description: Maryland school districts are defending their policies to protect transgender student athletes, despite a U.S. Department of Education charge this week that the policies may violate Title IX.

articles.title: Most Maryland congressional incumbents cruise to primary wins

articles.title: U.S. Rep. April McClain Delaney projected to win Maryland 6th Congressional District
articles.description: After a bitter, expensive and largely self-funded campaign, freshman U.S. Rep. April McClain Delaney (D-6th) has held her seat against David Trone, who was trying to reclaim it after a two year absence.

articles.title: Don’t bet on it: State eyes action against election ‘trading’ on prediction markets
articles.description: Maryland residents wagering on the outcome of elections using online prediction markets could find themselves on the wrong side of election law and potentially face criminal prosecution, state officials say.

articles.title: Federal judge tosses Justice Department lawsuit seeking Maryland voter records
articles.description: A federal district judge has dismissed a Justice Department lawsuit demanding Maryland voter registration records, what state officials derided as a "fishing expedition" for sensitive personal information on voters.

articles.title: Maryland Revolutionary War soldier’s remains identified in South Carolina
articles.description: A Texas-based forensics company has solved a vexing mystery, identifying the long-buried remains of a Revolutionary War soldier found in South Carolina — who turned out to be a teenager from Anne Arundel County.

articles.title: Legislative candidates’ biggest challenge? The state’s budget outlook
articles.description: Candidates for the Maryland General Assembly have all kinds of ideas for the state’s future, but one thing could stand in the way of any grand plans: the state's looming — and widening — budget shortfalls.

articles.title: House sets target dates for special session
articles.description: Members of the House of Delegates are being asked to consider two windows for a special session that could consider two possible amendments to the Maryland Constitution.

articles.title: Supreme Court rejects Maryland woman’s attempt to press state, federal cases
articles.description: A divided Supreme Court Thursday said a Maryland woman cannot press a lawsuit against the University of Maryland Medical System while her state court appeal of the same issue is still pending.

articles.title: In state races across Maryland, it’s all about Trump as primary looms
articles.description: President Donald Trump stands at the center of everything in Washington these days — and as voters prepare to vote in Maryland’s primaries next week, he seems to be at the center there, too.

articles.title: USDA approves disaster declaration for Maryland farms over late spring freeze
articles.description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture on Monday approved the state's request for a disaster declaration after a late-April freeze devastated some orchards and vineyards in the state.

articles.title: Surging inflation making it harder for Marylanders to make ends meet
articles.description: Inflation surged last month to the highest rate in more than three years, driving up the costs of everything from food to gas for Maryland families, experts said, and putting aid groups on edge.
